title: pyrus tanpaensis
parts: Pyrus tanpaensis has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, roots, and bark, which are utilized to create various remedies. The leaves of Pyrus tanpaensis contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of the plant are rich in essential oils, including geraniol and linalool, which have been used to treat anxiety and insomnia. The roots of Pyrus tanpaensis, on the other hand, contain iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and digestive issues.
side_effects: Pyrus tanpaensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Some examples of these side effects include diarrhea, hives, and difficulty breathing. Overuse or incorrect preparation of the plant can also lead to liver damage and kidney failure in severe cases. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women may experience uterine contractions and other complications if they consume Pyrus tanpaensis without proper guidance.
